title: Construction Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Scheduling, budgeting, RFI tracking and progress reporting are substantially digital and automatable, but on-site coordination, safety judgment and subcontractor negotiation are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-enabled project-management and BIM-coordination platforms (scheduling optimization, cost forecasting, clash detection) are credible and improving fast, short of full maturity.
rationales.adoption: Mid-size and large general contractors have adopted AI-assisted scheduling and cost-forecasting tools; small residential builders mostly have not.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can draft schedules, flag budget overruns and route RFIs, a real workflow slice, while site safety calls and subcontractor disputes stay human.

tasksGrowing: On-site safety judgment and incident response
Adapting schedules to real-world site surprises (weather, supply delays)
salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 11-9021, p25-p75 rounded, upper end extended toward large commercial project postings

defenseSkills.skill: AI-assisted scheduling and cost-forecasting platform fluency

insight: Construction management is the highest-exposure role in this category because the job is half digital pipeline: schedules, budgets, RFIs and progress reports are exactly what AI project-management tools now handle, and mid-size and large contractors are already running them. What still requires a human is the part no software owns: reading a job site, negotiating with subcontractors, and making the safety call when the plan and reality diverge.
